text: What kind of fish are they?
Fish and chips has always been a favourite dish in Britain,
zh: 炸鱼薯条一直是英国人最喜爱的菜肴,

text: but as the oceans have been overfished, fish has become more and more expensive.
zh: 但随着海洋过度捕捞,鱼的价格越来越贵。

text: So it comes as a surprise to learn that giant fish are terrifying the divers on North Sea oil rigs.
zh: 所以听到北海石油钻井平台上的潜水员被巨型鱼吓坏了,真让人意外。

text: Oil rigs have to be repaired frequently and divers, who often have to work in darkness a hundred feet under water,
zh: 石油钻井平台需要经常维修,潜水员常常要在水下一百英尺深的黑暗中作业,

text: have been frightened out of their wits by giant fish bumping into them as they work.
zh: 工作时被巨型鱼撞到,吓得魂飞魄散。

text: Now they have had special cages made to protect them from these monsters.
zh: 现在他们特制了防护笼,以防这些庞然大物的伤害。

text: The fish are not sharks or killer whales,
zh: 这些鱼既不是鲨鱼,也不是虎鲸,

text: but favourite eating varieties like cod and skate which grow to unnatural sizes,
zh: 而是像鳕鱼和鳐鱼这样常见的食用鱼类,只是长得异常巨大,

text: sometimes as much as twelve feet in length.
zh: 有时体长可达十二英尺。

text: Three factors have caused these fish to grow so large:
zh: 有三个因素导致这些鱼长得如此巨大:

text: the warm water round the hot oil pipes under the sea;
zh: 海底热油管周围的温暖海水;

text: the plentiful supply of food thrown overboard by the crews on the rigs;
zh: 钻井平台工作人员抛入海中的充足食物;

text: the total absence of fishing boats around the oil rigs.
zh: 钻井平台附近完全没有渔船捕捞。

text: As a result, the fish just eat and eat and grow and grow in the lovely warm water.
zh: 结果,这些鱼在温暖宜人的海水里不停地吃,不停地长。
